name: North Rhine-Westphalia (Germany)

colors.symbolism: from the historical colours of Westphalia
shared by Westphalia and the Rhineland
from the historical colours of the Rhineland

description: Three equal horizontal stripes of green, white, and red.
history: Adopted on 10 March 1953 as the civil flag of the state of North Rhine-Westphalia, formed in 1946 from the Prussian provinces of the Rhineland and Westphalia. The state flag (with arms) is reserved for official use.

features.symbolism: Green and white from the arms of Westphalia (the Saxon horse on a white-and-green field) combined with the red and white of the Rhineland.
